# homebuycheck.com
> Free home affordability calculator. Find out how much house you can afford based on income, debts, and down payment.

## What this tool does
- Calculates maximum home purchase price based on the 28/36 DTI rule
- Front-end ratio: housing costs should not exceed 28% of gross monthly income
- Back-end ratio: total debt should not exceed 36% of gross monthly income
- Accounts for down payment, property taxes, HOA, and insurance
- Covers all 50 states

## How it works
The 28/36 rule is the standard used by most conventional mortgage lenders. The calculator finds the lower of:
1. The maximum home price where PITI stays under 28% of gross income
2. The maximum home price where all debt payments stay under 36% of gross income

## Key facts
- Every $1,000/month in existing debt reduces your max home price by roughly $150,000-$200,000
- A 20% down payment eliminates PMI, saving ~$100-$200/month on a $400K home
- At a 7% mortgage rate, a $500K home requires approximately $180,000 annual income to qualify comfortably

## Tool URL
https://homebuycheck.com

## Related tools
- Full financial calculator suite: https://calcmoney.io
- Net worth percentile calculator: https://networthrank.com
- Salary percentile calculator: https://salaryfact.com
- Take-home pay calculator: https://netpaytool.com
